hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The output consists of two lines. The first line starts with 'text=' followed by the full URL string. The second line starts with 'text_without_password=' followed by the URL with userinfo removed.

context_pointers.vague_reference: check the reserved escape handling section
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The output treats characters like '~' and '?' as literal strings rather than URL-encoded sequences, effectively unparsed them back to '~' and '?'. Reveal lock.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_parse_encoded_references.json.description: Parse an encoded reference and print its structural URL components without decoding percent escapes.

cases.expected_output:
text=https://%75%73%65%72:%00%00%00%00@xn--bcher-kva.ch:8080/a/nice%20nice/./path/?zot=23%25&zut#frég
text_without_password=https://%75%73%65%72:@xn--bcher-kva.ch:8080/a/nice%20nice/./path/?zot=23%25&zut#frég
scheme=https
uses_authority=true
host=bücher.ch
port=8080
rooted_path=true
path=["a","nice nice",".","path",""]
query=[["zot","23%"],["zut",null]]
fragment=frég
userinfo=["user","\u0000\u0000\u0000\u0000"]

text=/?%61rg=b&arg=c
text_without_password=/?%61rg=b&arg=c
scheme=
uses_authority=false
host=
port=null
rooted_path=true
path=[""]
query=[["arg","b"],["arg","c"]]
fragment=
userinfo=[""]

feature1_3_parse_facade_and_lazy_decodin.description: Parse through the high-level parser, selecting decoded or encoded output and reporting deferred decoding failures as neutral errors.

feature1_4_reject_malformed_references.j.description: Reject malformed references and print a language-neutral error category plus the offending input.

feature2_1_append_path_segments.json.description: Append one or more literal path segments while preserving the existing query and fragment and escaping embedded separators.

feature2_2_replace_last_path_segment.jso.description: Replace the final path segment with a literal sibling segment while preserving query and fragment data.

feature2_3_resolve_relative_references.j.description: Resolve a relative reference against a base reference using standard dot-segment, query, fragment, and authority rules.

feature3_1_encoded_query_updates.json.description: Add, set, and remove encoded query parameters while preserving ordering and valueless parameters.

feature3_2_decoded_query_updates.json.description: Manipulate query parameters through decoded Unicode names and values while serializing them back to escaped text.

cases.expected_output:
text=http://bücher.ch/a b?x=é#frég
host=bücher.ch
path=["a b"]
query=[["x","é"]]
fragment=frég

feature4_1_uri_iri_conversion.json.description: Convert reference text between URI-safe ASCII form and IRI Unicode form while preserving component meaning.

feature4_2_normalize_references.json.description: Normalize references by canonicalizing scheme and host case, removing default ports, resolving dot segments, and normalizing safe escapes.

scheme=deltron
uses_authority=true
port=3030
host=example.com
path=[]

text=deltron://example.com
scheme=deltron
uses_authority=true
port=3030
host=example.com
path=[]

feature5_1_register_authority_schemes.js.description: Register an authority-based scheme with a default port and render URLs using its authority and default-port rules.

cases.expected_output:
text=noloctron:example/path
scheme=noloctron
uses_authority=false
port=null
host=
path=["example","path"]

feature5_2_register_path_only_schemes.js.description: Register a path-only scheme and render paths without an authority separator.

feature5_3_reject_invalid_scheme_registr.description: Reject inconsistent scheme registration metadata using language-neutral error output.

feature6_1_compare_reference_identity.js.description: Compare parsed references by normalized structural identity and report hash-set distinctness with their rendered text.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains a reference string and a feature action (e.g., 'inspect_encoded_url', 'inspect_decoded_url', 'parse_url')
_constraints.output_rule: Output must begin with 'representation=decoded' only if input field 'decoded' is true; otherwise 'representation=encoded'. Followed by 'text=' then component lines.

_constraints.input_condition: Parsed reference has a port that is not a valid integer (e.g., 'bad')
_constraints.output_rule: Must NOT attempt to parse components. Output must start with 'error=invalid_port' followed by 'url=' with the original input string.

_constraints.input_condition: Parsed reference has malformed authority/bracket syntax (e.g., starts with '[' but lacks closing ']')
_constraints.output_rule: Must NOT attempt to parse components. Output must start with 'error=invalid_url' followed by 'url=' with the original input string.

_constraints.input_condition: A path segment contains literal forward slashes (e.g., 'gong/double/')
_constraints.output_rule: The rendered 'text' must contain percent-encoded slashes ('%2F'). The printed 'path' JSON array must contain the segment with escaped slashes as strings (e.g., "gong%2Fdouble%2F").

_constraints.input_condition: A query update step has 'value' as null (or missing, resulting in default null handling)
_constraints.output_rule: The printed 'query' array must contain the pair with the value as explicit JSON null (e.g., "zut",null). The rendered 'text' for that pair must have no value (empty string after '=').

_constraints.input_condition: Multiple query updates are applied sequentially
_constraints.output_rule: The printed 'query' array must preserve the global insertion order. 'add' appends to the end; 'set' replaces all existing pairs with that name but maintains position relative to other keys; 'remove' deletes key but shifts subsequent keys down.

_constraints.input_condition: A 'feature' is called that parses or renders a URL (all Feature 1 features)
_constraints.output_rule: The first line must be 'text=' (followed by the full string). The second line must be 'text_without_password=' followed by the same string but with userinfo removed.

_constraints.input_condition: A valid URL reference is parsed
_constraints.output_rule: Component lines must be printed in this exact sequence: text, text_without_password, scheme, uses_authority, host, port, rooted_path, path (inline array), query (inline array), fragment, userinfo.

_constraints.input_condition: 'representation=encoded' is selected for query values
_constraints.output_rule: The 'query' JSON array and 'query_values' list must contain percent-encoded strings (e.g., '23%25').

_constraints.input_condition: 'representation=decoded' is selected for query values
_constraints.output_rule: The 'query' JSON array must contain decoded Unicode strings (e.g., '23%').

_constraints.input_condition: Input URL has uppercase scheme (e.g., 'HTTP://...')
_constraints.output_rule: The rendered 'text' and 'scheme' field must be lowercased ('http', 'HTTP').

_constraints.input_condition: Input URL has uppercase host (e.g., 'EXAMPLE.COM')
_constraints.output_rule: The rendered 'text' and 'host' field must be lowercased ('example.com').

_constraints.input_condition: Input path contains dot segments ('./', '../')
_constraints.output_rule: The rendered 'text' and 'path' array must resolve absolute path segments (remove '.' and '..' unless result is absolute root/empty).

_constraints.input_condition: Path contains escape sequences for reserved characters (e.g., '~', '?')
_constraints.output_rule: The rendered 'text' and 'path' array must treat these as literals (not escaped), effectively converting '%7E' to '~' and '%3F' to '?' in the output text.

_constraints.input_condition: Base URL is deep relative path (e.g., 'http://a/b/c/d;p?q') and reference is '..'
_constraints.output_rule: The 'path' array must correctly pop the last segment (replacing 'd' with the reference). Query retains its original comma-separated or list format.

_constraints.input_condition: Reference contains query string and fragment when base does not
_constraints.output_rule: Reference preserves its own query and fragment in the resolved output.

_constraints.input_condition: Reference is authority-relative (starts with '//')
_constraints.output_rule: Reference replaces the base authority (host/port). The base path, query, and fragment are preserved (with empty path if reference is purely authority).

_constraints.input_condition: 'query_key' parameter is provided alongside 'parse_url'
_constraints.output_rule: Output must append the line 'query_values=["value"]' after the fragment line.

_constraints.input_condition: 'read_fragment' parameter is provided alongside 'parse_url'
_constraints.output_rule: Output must append the line 'fragment_value=value' after the standard component block.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains IDN domain (e.g., 'xn--bcher-kva.ch')
_constraints.output_rule: If 'decoded=true', 'text' and 'host' render decoded (e.g., 'bücher.ch'). If 'decoded=false', 'text' and 'host' render encoded.

_constraints.input_condition: "set" change affects an existing key
_constraints.output_rule: All existing entries for that key are removed; a single new entry is added. The 'path' array is NOT modified by query changes.

_constraints.input_condition: Register scheme with 'uses_authority=false' and 'default_port' > 0
_constraints.output_rule: Reject immediately. Output must start with 'error=invalid_scheme_registration' followed by 'scheme=' with the provided scheme name.

_constraints.input_condition: Register scheme with 'uses_authority=null' (boolean missing/false) explicitly meaning no authority support
_constraints.output_rule: Reject immediately. Output must start with 'error=invalid_scheme_registration' followed by 'scheme=' with the provided scheme name.

_constraints.input_condition: Comparing two URL strings via 'compare_urls'
_constraints.output_rule: If outputs are equal structurally, 'equal=true' and 'distinct_hash_count=1'. If different, 'equal=false' and 'distinct_hash_count=2'. Output also lists 'first_text' and 'second_text'.

_constraints.input_condition: Input has default port (80 for http, 443 for https)
_constraints.output_rule: The 'port' field must be null or omitted (not 80 or 443) in the component block.

hide.oracle_response: The output consists of two lines. The first line starts with 'text=' followed by the full URL string. The second line starts with 'text_without_password=' followed by the URL with userinfo removed.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: check the reserved escape handling section
vague_refs.actual_meaning: The output treats characters like '~' and '?' as literal strings rather than URL-encoded sequences, effectively unparsed them back to '~' and '?'. Reveal lock.
